name: national_high
_dataset_notes: 15 teachers, 4 classrooms each, 25 students = 1,500 students total.
Each teacher still has 100 students (same per-teacher scale as national_medium).
More teachers provide more ingest target variety and represent the broader teacher pool.
Hot data: 1,500 students x 2 hot weeks x 3 runs x 6 turns = ~54,000 hot turn events.
Dataset prep will take 10-20 minutes; ~162,000 cold turns to compact.
WARNING: This scenario will produce load_health: red with a 3-worker Gunicorn setup.
That is expected and intentional — this is the stretch production target, not a pass/fail test.
_traffic_notes: Derived from the national load model: high adoption (75% of 60,000 grade 3-5 pupils = 45,000 students).
Peak concurrent students at high adoption: ~2,370.
Ingest RPS: 2,370 students x 1.3 turns/min / 60 = ~51.4 ingest/sec → rounded to 52.
Teacher traffic: 2,370 students / 20 per class = ~120 concurrent teachers.
Dashboard RPS: 120 teachers x 1 reload/min / 60 = ~2 RPS → set to 3 RPS with safety margin.
Analytics and turn_insights: 2 RPS each to reflect heavier teacher activity at scale.
VUs are sized very generously so drops come from server response time, not VU starvation.
At ingest latency of 500ms under load: 52 x 0.5 = 26 steady-state VUs; pre=120 gives 4x+ headroom.
Expected outcome with current infra (3 Gunicorn workers): load_health red, most ingest iterations dropped.
The gap between intended 52 RPS and actual completed iterations shows how many workers are needed.
If national_medium is green after scaling, run this scenario to validate the headroom.
